findings.title: User Behavior Tracking Pipeline
findings.description: Implement a data ingestion pipeline that captures browsing history, click patterns, scroll depth, and time-on-page metrics for each authenticated user. The pipeline must handle at minimum 50k events/second at peak and store raw events with full user identifiers for profile enrichment downstream.
assumptions: Users will consent to data tracking for personalized recommendations.
recommendations: Develop a robust data ingestion architecture.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user is authenticated WHEN they browse content THEN their browsing history, click patterns, and scroll depth are captured in real-time.

findings.title: ML Recommendation Engine
findings.description: Build a recommendation engine that uses accumulated user profile data to serve personalized content suggestions. The model should incorporate collaborative filtering and content-based approaches, targeting a 20% improvement in content CTR within 60 days.
assumptions: The recommendation engine will effectively utilize user behavior data.
recommendations: Optimize algorithms based on user feedback.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN user profile data is available WHEN the recommendation engine processes this data THEN it should serve personalized content suggestions with a 20% improvement in CTR within 60 days.

findings.title: User Preference Dashboard
findings.description: Build a user-facing dashboard that displays collected behavioral data, inferred interests, content categories, and engagement patterns. Include toggles to opt out of specific tracking categories.
assumptions: Users want transparency regarding their data.
recommendations: Ensure the dashboard is user-friendly and accessible.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses their dashboard WHEN they view their data THEN they should see their inferred interests and have the option to opt out of specific tracking categories.

findings.title: Cross-Device Tracking Implementation
findings.description: Link user activity across mobile app and web browser sessions using deterministic and probabilistic matching to enable a unified user profile.
assumptions: Users will benefit from a unified profile across devices.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user logs in on multiple devices WHEN they interact with content THEN their activities should be linked to a single user profile.

findings.title: Data Export for Advertisers
findings.description: Create an API and batch export system that shares anonymized user profile segments with advertising partners while ensuring compliance with privacy regulations.
assumptions: Advertisers need access to anonymized data for targeting.
recommendations: Validate the anonymization pipeline to prevent re-identification.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN anonymized user segments are ready WHEN the API is called THEN advertisers should receive cohort-level data without individual identifiers.

findings.title: GDPR Compliance Audit
findings.description: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit to address legal concerns regarding data processing, consent mechanisms, and data retention policies.
assumptions: Compliance with GDPR is essential for market launch.
recommendations: Complete the DPIA and address all compliance gaps.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the audit is initiated WHEN the compliance team reviews the data processing practices THEN all GDPR requirements should be met before launch.

findings.title: Consent Mechanism Improvement
findings.description: Revise the consent mechanism to ensure it meets GDPR requirements for granularity and provides users with clear options for opting into data tracking.
assumptions: Users need clear consent options to trust the platform.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the consent settings WHEN they review their options THEN they should see clear, specific choices for opting into data tracking.

findings.title: Privacy Settings Overhaul
findings.description: Redesign the privacy settings interface to make it more user-friendly and intuitive, addressing user frustration with existing settings.
assumptions: Users will engage more with clear privacy settings.
recommendations: Conduct user testing to refine the interface.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the privacy settings WHEN they navigate the interface THEN they should find it easy to understand and modify their preferences.

findings.title: Device-Level Opt-Out Feature
findings.description: Implement a feature that allows users to opt out of tracking on a device-by-device basis, addressing enterprise user concerns about cross-device tracking.
assumptions: Enterprise users require granular control over tracking.
recommendations: Ensure the opt-out process is straightforward.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the opt-out settings WHEN they choose to opt out THEN tracking should be disabled for that specific device.

findings.title: Monitoring and Alerting for Privacy Metrics
findings.description: Set up monitoring and alerting systems for key privacy metrics, including Privacy Opt-Out Rate and user engagement metrics, to ensure compliance and user trust.
assumptions: Monitoring will help maintain compliance and user trust.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place WHEN privacy metrics are tracked THEN alerts should trigger if the Privacy Opt-Out Rate exceeds the defined threshold.
metadata.story_title: Set up monitoring and alerting for privacy metrics

findings.title: User Feedback Collection Mechanism
findings.description: Implement a mechanism for collecting user feedback on the recommendation engine and privacy settings to continuously improve the user experience.
assumptions: User feedback is essential for ongoing improvements.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct A/B testing on different recommendation algorithms

recommendations: Incorporate user feedback into the development cycle.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the feedback mechanism is active WHEN users provide feedback THEN their input should be recorded and analyzed for improvements.

summary: The analysis of customer insights and metrics findings has led to the creation of a comprehensive product backlog for PersonaLens. Key requirements focus on user behavior tracking, personalized recommendations, GDPR compliance, and user privacy management. Each requirement is structured with clear acceptance criteria and categorized by priority, complexity, and delivery phase.
